highlights.title: Revolut CEO Storonsky Says Digital Bank’s IPO Is Two Years Out
highlights.description: Nik Storonsky, Revolut co-founder and CEO, said he wants to take the London-based digital bank public, but not until at least 2028, extending the timeline on one of Europe’s most anticipated offerings. “Two years away,” Storonsky said regarding a potential initial public offering in an interview with David Rubenstein for an upcoming episode of "The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Siemens Warns EU’s AI Rules Will Deter Investment in Europe
highlights.description: Siemens AG will prioritize artificial-intelligence investments in the US and China if the European Union doesn’t adapt its restrictive regulations, according to Chief Executive Officer Roland Busch.

highlights.title: Asia Regulators Raise Scrutiny on Banks Amid Mythos AI Fears
highlights.description: Regulators across Asia are stepping up scrutiny of cybersecurity risks in their financial systems, as concerns over Anthropic PBC’s latest AI model Mythos spread.

highlights.title: China’s Netflix iQiyi Goes All-In on AI Content in Big Overhaul
highlights.description: IQiyi Inc. expects AI to create a big chunk of its films and shows virtually from scratch someday soon, a monumental industry shift that spurred the Netflix-style streaming service to begin the biggest corporate overhaul in its 16-year history.

highlights.title: AI’s Token Economy Revolution Creates New China Tech Winners
highlights.description: China’s cheap artificial intelligence models are rapidly attracting global users and creating new winners in the nation’s stock market.
